display.purpose: Page-level titles, modal headings, the top of any primary view.

headline.purpose: Section headings, dialog headers, detail sheet titles.

title.purpose: Table column headings used as sections, card subtitles, sidebar section labels.

body.purpose: Table cells, form values, description text. Max 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Form field labels, status pills, badge text, metadata chips.

shadows.name: ambient-low
shadows.purpose: Default card at rest. Lifts the card off the body surface. The minimum structural signal.

shadows.name: ambient-mid
shadows.purpose: Interactive card hover. Shows responsiveness; the card answers the cursor.

shadows.name: structural
shadows.purpose: Sheets, drawers, open dropdowns. Surfaces that have entered the scene above content.

shadows.name: heavy-lift
shadows.purpose: Modals and dialogs. The top of the z-stack.

shadows.name: neon-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary action elements only. The green activation aura. Never on neutral surfaces.

shadows.name: neon-gold
shadows.purpose: Primary card variant. A softer, downward version of the glow. Directional warmth from the green.

shadows.name: dark-mid
shadows.purpose: Dark theme equivalent of ambient-mid. Opacity must be higher to read against near-black surfaces.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default state transitions. Color, opacity, shadow changes.

motion.name: ease-sheet
motion.purpose: Sheet/drawer entry. Minimal overshoot, purposeful deceleration.

motion.name: ease-exit
motion.purpose: Sheet/drawer exit. Fast initial movement, then resolves.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Standard state changes: color, background, border.

motion.name: duration-enter
motion.purpose: Elements entering the scene: fade-in, slide-up, sheet entry.

motion.name: duration-exit
motion.purpose: Elements leaving: sheet exit. Slightly longer so the motion resolves fully.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.purpose: Mobile-first breakpoint. Below this: single-column, bottom nav.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Tablet: two-column layouts begin, bottom nav may shift to sidebar.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Desktop: sidebar fully visible, content area expands.

breakpoints.name: 2xl
breakpoints.purpose: Container max-width cap. Nothing expands beyond this.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main CTA. Used for save, confirm, create. Neon signal green with ink text.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; background-color: #00E5A0; color: #0D0D12; border: 1.5px solid rgba(0,229,160,0.6); border-radius: 0.4rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease, transform 0.1s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background-color: #00C98C; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #F7F8FA, 0 0 0 4px #00E5A0; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral action. Cancel, back, secondary confirm. Surface tile background.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; background-color: #F0F1F5; color: #0D0D12; border: 1.5px solid #E4E4E7; border-radius: 0.4rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background-color: rgba(240,241,245,0.75); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #F7F8FA, 0 0 0 4px #00E5A0; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Low-emphasis action. Often used inline with icons. Transparent until hover.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-ghost"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M3 6h18"/><path d="M19 6v14c0 1-1 2-2 2H7c-1 0-2-1-2-2V6"/><path d="M8 6V4c0-1 1-2 2-2h4c1 0 2 1 2 2v2"/></svg> Eliminar</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; background-color: transparent; color: #5A5A72; border: 1.5px solid transparent; border-radius: 0.4rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background-color: #F0F1F5; color: #0D0D12; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #F7F8FA, 0 0 0 4px #00E5A0; }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Delete and irreversible actions. Signal crimson. Always paired with a confirmation step.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 8px 16px; background-color: #FF4D6A; color: #ffffff; border: 1.5px solid rgba(255,77,106,0.6); border-radius: 0.4rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background-color: rgba(255,77,106,0.9); } .ds-btn-destructive:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #F7F8FA, 0 0 0 4px #FF4D6A; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field. Surface tile background, primary border on focus, 46px height.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; width: 100%; } .ds-label { font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; color: #0D0D12; letter-spacing: 0.01em; } .ds-input { display: flex; height: 46px; width: 100%; padding: 12px 14px; background-color: #F0F1F5; color: #0D0D12; border: 1.5px solid #E4E4E7; border-radius: 0.4rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; transition: border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #5A5A72; font-weight: 400; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #00E5A0; } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Full-pill label for categories, tags, and states. Four semantic variants.
components.html:
Activo
Completado
Cancelado
Pendiente
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid transparent; padding: 2px 10px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-primary { background-color: #00E5A0; color: #0D0D12; } .ds-badge-success { background-color: rgba(0,229,160,0.12); color: #006845; border-color: rgba(0,229,160,0.3); } .ds-badge-error { background-color: rgba(255,77,106,0.12); color: #cc0035; border-color: rgba(255,77,106,0.3); } .ds-badge-warning { background-color: rgba(255,184,48,0.12); color: #996500; border-color: rgba(255,184,48,0.3); }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Flat-corner pill with a dot indicator. Used inside tables for record status. 11px bold.
components.html: ActivoPendienteCancelado
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 0.4rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; } .ds-pill-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-pill-active { background-color: rgba(0,229,160,0.12); color: #006845; } .ds-pill-active .ds-pill-dot { background-color: #00E5A0; } .ds-pill-pending { background-color: rgba(255,184,48,0.12); color: #996500; } .ds-pill-pending .ds-pill-dot { background-color: #FFB830; } .ds-pill-cancelled { background-color: rgba(255,77,106,0.12); color: #cc0035; } .ds-pill-cancelled .ds-pill-dot { background-color: #FF4D6A; }

components.name: Sidebar Navigation
components.description: Dark rail nav. Always dark regardless of content theme. Active item: neon green bg with ink text.

components.name: Dialog Action Bar
components.description: Pinned footer action row inside dialogs and sheets. Flex row of outline buttons (h-12, 0.4rem radius, flex-1 each). Icon-only on mobile when 4+ actions; icon+label on desktop when 3 or fewer. Tone variants: primary (green tint), destructive (red tint), warning (amber tint). Overflow menu (MoreHorizontal) for secondary actions.
components.css: .ds-action-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; width: 100%; } .ds-action-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; height: 48px; flex: 1; padding: 0 8px; border-radius: 0.4rem; border: 1.5px solid #E4E4E7; background: #FFFFFF; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-action-icon { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 48px; width: 48px; flex-shrink: 0; border-radius: 0.4rem; border: 1.5px solid #E4E4E7; background: #FFFFFF; color: #5A5A72; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-action-icon:hover { background: #F0F1F5; } .ds-action-default { color: #0D0D12; } .ds-action-default:hover { background: #F0F1F5; } .ds-action-primary { border-color: rgba(0,229,160,0.25); background: rgba(0,229,160,0.10); color: #006845; } .ds-action-primary:hover { background: rgba(0,229,160,0.15); } .ds-action-destructive { border-color: rgba(255,77,106,0.20); background: rgba(255,77,106,0.10); color: #cc0035; } .ds-action-destructive:hover { background: rgba(255,77,106,0.15); }

narrative.overview:
This is a tool built to a tight tolerance. Every element has a job: nothing decorates, nothing gestures at personality without delivering function. The dot-matrix grid on the body surface is the one allowed flourish, and it earns its place by evoking something between a calibration sheet and a security terminal. The neon green accent is the activation signal: it fires on interactive targets and success states, not backgrounds, not text, not decoration.

The system supports both light and dark themes with equal seriousness. Light mode is used in a well-lit shop during the day; the nearly-black sidebar rail anchors the navigation against the pale surface. Dark mode is used when ambient light is low or when the user prefers it. The neon green reads clearly in both contexts.

This system explicitly rejects: the generic SaaS white-and-blue corporate look; dated desktop-software aesthetics with chrome-heavy modals and XP-era table styling; consumer-grade friendliness with oversized buttons, emoji, and hand-holding copy; complex enterprise UI with icon-only navigation rails packed with twenty items; and POS system aesthetics with garish color bands and receipt-printer legacy patterns.
keyCharacteristics: Single accent color (neon green) used at most 10-15% of any screen surface
Structural shadow system: depth communicates hierarchy, not mood
Flat border radius (0.4rem) across all components: tight and resolved, not aggressively rounded
DM Sans at high weight contrast: 800 for display, 400 for body
Dark sidebar rail in both themes; content area adapts to light/dark independently
Dot-matrix body texture as the only ambient decoration

rules.name: The One-Signal Rule
rules.body: The neon green accent appears on at most 10-15% of any given screen. It marks interactive targets and positive outcomes. When everything is green, nothing is.

rules.name: The Dark-Rail Anchor Rule
rules.body: The sidebar is always dark, in both themes. It is a permanent structural element. Do not invert or theme it with content-area palette changes.

rules.name: The Weight-Contrast Rule
rules.body: Minimum 1.4x weight contrast between adjacent hierarchy levels. Never use two adjacent levels at the same weight.

rules.name: The Tight-Heading Rule
rules.body: All headings use -0.02em letter-spacing. Wide-set headings at high weight are a consumer-app signal, not a tool signal.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces start flat. Shadows appear as responses to state (hover) or position (dialogs above cards above body). Never add a shadow to make something look nicer.

rules.name: The Glow-Is-Primary Rule
rules.body: The neon glow shadow is reserved for elements styled with the primary accent. It must not appear on neutral surfaces, secondary buttons, or informational cards.

dos: Do use the neon green exclusively on primary buttons, active nav items, success states, and focus rings.
Do use structural shadows to communicate hierarchy: body < card < sheet < modal.
Do keep all border radii at 0.4rem. A single radius across the system is part of the precision-instrument identity.
Do use DM Sans 800 for all page-level headings with -0.02em letter-spacing and tight line-height.
Do make the sidebar dark in both light and dark themes. It is a permanent structural anchor.
Do use Surface Tile (#F0F1F5) for input backgrounds and nested sub-surfaces inside cards.
Do apply the dot-matrix body texture only on the ground plane. Card backgrounds must be opaque to block it.
Do ensure all tap targets are at minimum 44px in height or width on mobile.
Do use table layouts for multi-record data. Cards are for single-record summaries only.
donts: Don't use the neon green as a large background fill, section tint, or decorative band.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient). The .gradient-text utility is legacy; do not extend it.
Don't expand the .glass glassmorphism pattern to new components.
Don't add a second accent color family to compete with neon green.
Don't use three-panel layouts, icon-only sidebar variants with more than 8 items, or nested navigation trees.
Don't use oversized buttons (height > 56px), emoji in UI copy, or excessive rounded corners (radius > 0.75rem).
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored stripe on cards or list items.
Don't rely on color alone to communicate state.
Don't design screens that look like a dated desktop application.
